bayesroc 0.1.0

    • Analyzing univariate recognition data with bayesroc
  • Changelog

Pratte et al. (2010) word recognition memory data

prm09.Rd

Trial-level word-recognition data from Pratte et al. (2010). 97 subjects each studied 240 words and were tested on 240 studied + 240 lure words, rating each item on a 6-point confidence scale ("sure new" to "sure studied").

Usage

prm09

Format

A data frame with 46,495 rows and 5 columns:

cond

integer, 0 = lure (new), 1 = studied (old). Use as the is_old indicator in formulas.

sub

integer subject ID (0-96, 97 subjects).

item

integer item ID (0-479).

lag

numeric study-test lag (only meaningful for studied items; 0 for lures).

resp

integer 0-5 confidence rating (0 = "sure new", 5 = "sure studied").

Source

Pratte, M. S., Rouder, J. N., & Morey, R. D. (2010). Separating mnemonic process from participant and item effects in the assessment of ROC asymmetries. Journal of Experimental Psychology: Learning, Memory, and Cognition, 36(1), 224-232. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0017750

Details

Confidence is coded 0..5 in the bundled data; bayesroc auto-shifts to 1..6 for fitting (with a one-line message).

On this page
